title: Sour Flaky Middle Eastern Lamb Tagine
description: A hearty, comforting tagine that's perfect for a sick day, with a tangy and flaky crust.

ingredients.name: Lardons
ingredients.notes: You can use store-bought or make your own.

ingredients.name: Curry powder
ingredients.notes: Use a mild or hot curry powder depending on your preference.

ingredients.name: Flaky pastry
ingredients.notes: You can use store-bought or make your own.

instructions: Heat the oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the lardons and cook, stirring occasionally, until browned, about 5 minutes.
Remove the lardons from the pot and set them aside.
Add the onion to the pot and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic, ginger, cumin, curry powder, salt, and pepper to the pot and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the lamb to the pot and cook, stirring occasionally, until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the browned lardons, lemon juice, and 2 cups of water to the pot, then bring to a boil.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and cook, covered, for 2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Roll out the pastry on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Cut the pastry into a circle or rectangle shape, depending on your preference, to fit over the tagine.
Trim the edges of the pastry and brush the rim with the beaten egg.
Place the pastry over the tagine and press the edges to seal.
Brush the top of the pastry with the unsalted butter and cut a few slits in the top to allow steam to escape.
Bake the tagine in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and flaky.
Let the tagine rest for 10 minutes before serving.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve hot.
